codex:
## Verdict: IMPROVE

The paper is not maximized. Its strongest result is currently hidden by an unnecessarily restrictive scalar tie-randomization convention. Finite LP duality gives an always-existing least-favorable mixture once boundary randomization may vary by assignment point. The prose also materially understates the resolved result and contains one endpoint error.

Formula (A) is false at its endpoint as written. Its right-hand side includes alpha=1, whereas A_n is a subset of (0,1).

A mixture P_lambda of product null vertices generally is not itself in M_0,n, because mixing can destroy conditional independence. It lies in conv(V_n).

Support can be restricted to Q-monotone vertices under the frozen product-order assumptions, but this needs an explicit complete-class lemma; it does not follow from duality alone.

Every finite matched-set game admits a level-indexed least-favorable mixture and a generalized Neyman-Pearson saddle rule; ordinary scalar tie randomization can fail even when a single Q-monotone null vertex is least favorable.

The common-Gamma design-sensitivity envelope is not derivable under the frozen primitives. The two-sided strict-improvement region is also not derivable. The strongest honest target under exactly the same assumptions is Gamma -> beta_n^star(alpha;Q,Gamma,r) as an exact finite conditional common-Gamma sensitivity envelope, computable by the primal/dual LP, with the pair specialization reducing to the classical McNemar-Cox extreme-tilt frontier.
why: Codex found a concrete stronger honest theorem under the same assumptions plus required de-laundering/prose fixes, so D0 must iterate before D0.5. Do not resume forward until the generalized tie-randomization saddle and monotone support reduction are discharged and prose is synchronized.

Define coordinatewise monotonicity explicitly. Provide exact primal and dual certificates for both LPs. The independently verified optima are phi_all=((0,0,0),(0,0,1),(0,1/2,3/4),(0,1,1)), beta_all=143/240, and phi_mon=((0,0,0),(0,1/3,5/6),(0,5/12,5/6),(0,11/12,1)), beta_mon=107/180, so the exact loss is 1/720. Merely reporting this in Technical internal limitation does not prove the asserted false-complete-class result.

2. Repair validity-region topology. Each tie-block projection is a closed interval in ambient [0,1]; the actual region is its union intersected with (0,1). Thus components touching 0 or 1 may be relatively closed or half-open, as illustrated by (0,2/3]. Amend theorem and proof.

The frozen frontier optimizes directly over all randomized tests and defines neither a statistic-choice class nor a statistic-to-test mapping. It upper-bounds every specified statistic family, but is not formally a statistic-choice optimum. Likewise, call the pair result an all-tests likelihood-ratio/pair reduction compatible with the McNemar–Cox boundary unless an explicit equivalence is defined and proved.

5. Correct all stale m=3 prose. The verified robust optimizer is (1/10,7/10,1), beta*=197/260, with convexified dual mixture weights 16/21 on v+=(1/4,1/4,1/2) and 5/21 on v_{2,3}=(1/5,2/5,2/5). The finite primal/dual formulation, c=sum y>0, normalization of lambda, P_lambda phi=alpha, binding supported vertices, generalized NP implications, D_B=D_V support criterion, full-vertex convexified-mixture existence, and finite extreme-point affine envelope are otherwise valid. The note also correctly avoids treating a nondegenerate P_lambda as an independent Rosenbaum null. No stronger automatic Q-monotone-support theorem is available under the same assumptions, as the verified counterexample demonstrates.

D0 SOLVER DIRECTIVE — [STRUCTURED CORE CHANGES REQUIRED; REBUILD REVERSE DEPENDENCIES; SYNC PROSE AND RENDER]

Resolve all five D0.5 findings through finite proto/core/prose revisions, without changing any proved mathematics except deleting redundant premises:

1. In `thm:pair-lf-frontier`, delete `ass:causal-likelihood-order` from both the displayed theorem statement and `depends_on`; rebuild `ass:causal-likelihood-order.used_by` accordingly. Retain `ass:causal-likelihood-separation`. For pairs,
\[
Q_i(2;r_i)/Q_i(1;r_i)>\Gamma_i\ge1
\]
and positivity imply \(Q_i(2;r_i)>Q_i(1;r_i)\), so strict likelihood order is derived. The proof remains unchanged substantively: equation (2) uses separation directly. Do not remove the order assumption globally because other results still invoke it.

2. In `thm:set-mixture-lp-characterization`, replace the opening
“Suppose \(\max_i m_i\ge3\). Under … `ass:causal-likelihood-separation` …”
by
“Under … `ass:causal-likelihood-order` …”
with no size restriction and no separation assumption. Delete `ass:causal-likelihood-separation` from this theorem’s `depends_on`, rebuild its `used_by`, and retain all other dependencies. The proof needs only \(q_z>0\), the fixed subset \(\mathcal B=\mathcal V_n^Q(r)\), vertex reduction, and finite LP duality. Preserve the \(n=1,m=3\) calculation verbatim as a worked support-switch example—not a theorem premise—including \(\mathcal A_1=(0,2/3]\), \(\phi^\star=(1/10,7/10,1)\), \(197/260\), and weights \(16/21,5/21\).

3. Repair novelty positioning by conceding the generic part. State that existence of a level-specific least-favorable convexified null mixture and a generalized Neyman–Pearson saddle rule is a finite LP/minimax consequence and is not itself the field-level advance. Huber–Strassen prove a fixed representative-pair Neyman–Pearson result when the composite hypotheses are described by order-2 alternating capacities; the note has not shown that the Rosenbaum product-law upper envelope satisfies that capacity hypothesis, and its level-dependent support switches preclude presenting Huber–Strassen as a direct fixed-pair corollary. Describe the result as a finite Rosenbaum-specific specialization: explicit product-vertex constraints, dual support criterion \(D_{\mathcal B}=D_{\mathcal V}\), exact classical-validity topology, and certified support-switch examples. Position matched-sensitivity comparators accurately: Rosenbaum (1988) treats a selected generalized signed-rank family; Fogarty–Small (2016) uses a quadratically constrained linear program to enforce common hidden-assignment probabilities across multiple outcomes and obtain familywise-error improvements; Cohen–Olson–Fogarty (2020) optimizes over coherent linear combinations of prespecified statistics and develops asymptotic \(\bar\chi^2\)/design-sensitivity theory. None is to be described as optimizing this fixed-\(Q\), finite conditional frontier over every randomized rejection rule. Primary-source scope is supported by [Huber–Strassen](https://doi.org/10.1214/aos/1176342363), [Fogarty–Small](https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.2015.1120675), [Cohen–Olson–Fogarty](https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/asaa024), and [Rosenbaum](https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/75.3.577).

Add/replace these exact bibliography entries:

- `HuberStrassen1973Minimax`: “Huber, P. J. and Strassen, V. (1973). Minimax tests and the Neyman--Pearson lemma for capacities. \emph{The Annals of Statistics}, 1(2), 251--263. doi:10.1214/aos/1176342363.”
- `FogartySmall2016MultipleComparisons`: “Fogarty, C. B. and Small, D. S. (2016). Sensitivity analysis for multiple comparisons in matched observational studies through quadratically constrained linear programming. \emph{Journal of the American Statistical Association}, 111(516), 1820--1830. doi:10.1080/01621459.2015.1120675.”
- `CohenOlsonFogarty2020AdversarialGame`: “Cohen, P. L., Olson, M. A., and Fogarty, C. B. (2020). Multivariate one-sided testing in matched observational studies as an adversarial game. \emph{Biometrika}, 107(4), 809--825. doi:10.1093/biomet/asaa024.”
- `Rosenbaum1988SensitivityMultipleControls`: “Rosenbaum, P. R. (1988). Sensitivity analysis for matching with multiple controls. \emph{Biometrika}, 75(3), 577--581. doi:10.1093/biomet/75.3.577.”

Use this concise differentiation language in Related Work and the theorem metadata: “Generic finite minimax duality supplies least-favorable convexified nulls; the Rosenbaum-specific contribution here is the explicit product-vertex LP, support-restriction criterion, classical-validity region, and exact support-switch certificates. Huber--Strassen’s stronger fixed representative-pair conclusion requires an alternating-capacity structure not established here. Fogarty--Small and Cohen--Olson--Fogarty solve constrained programs over multiple outcomes or coherent combinations of prespecified statistics, rather than the present finite conditional optimization over all randomized tests.”

The structural repair is correct. It adds a genuine asymptotic limit-and-rate theorem beyond the finite LP characterization and plausibly clears the Stat field floor when presented as an oracle all-randomized-tests design-sensitivity benchmark—not as new concentration machinery or a practical statistic-selection procedure.

Two corrections strengthen the proposed claim:

- At \(\Gamma\ge \widetilde\Gamma^\star\), the frontier equals \(\alpha\), not merely \(\le\alpha\), because the constant randomized test \(\phi\equiv\alpha\) is feasible.
- “Local alternative” should mean “one-set alternative”; it is fixed across replicas, not contiguous or \(n^{-1/2}\)-local.

## Mathematical adjudication

Let
\[
\mathcal P_m(\Gamma)
=\left\{p\in\Delta_m:p_j\le \Gamma p_k\ \forall j,k\right\},
\qquad
q_j=\frac{Q(j)}{\sum_kQ(k)},
\]
where \(0<Q(1)\le\cdots\le Q(m)\) and \(Q(1)<Q(m)\). Then
\[
q\in\mathcal P_m(\Gamma)
\iff \max_{j,k}\frac{q_j}{q_k}\le\Gamma
\iff \frac{Q(m)}{Q(1)}\le\Gamma.
\]
Thus the boundary direction is exactly:
\[
\Gamma<\frac{Q(m)}{Q(1)} \Longrightarrow q\notin\mathcal P_m(\Gamma),
\qquad
\Gamma\ge\frac{Q(m)}{Q(1)} \Longrightarrow q\in\mathcal P_m(\Gamma).
\]

For fixed sub-boundary \(\Gamma\), compact convex strict separation supplies \(s\in\mathbb R^m\) with
\[
\mu_1:=q^\top s
>
\mu_0:=\sup_{p\in\mathcal P_m(\Gamma)}p^\top s,
\qquad
\Delta:=\mu_1-\mu_0>0.
\]
Because the state space is finite, \(s\) is bounded. Put \(R_s=\max_j s_j-\min_j s_j>0\) and use
\[
\phi_n
=\mathbf1\left\{\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n s(Z_i)
\ge \mu_0+\frac{\Delta}{2}\right\}.
\]
For every independent, potentially non-identically distributed null
\[
P=\bigotimes_{i=1}^n p_i,\qquad p_i\in\mathcal P_m(\Gamma),
\]
Hoeffding gives the uniform bound
\[
\sup_P E_P\phi_n
\le
\exp\left\{-\frac{n\Delta^2}{2R_s^2}\right\}.
\]
Under the i.i.d. alternative \(q^{\otimes n}\),
\[
E_{q^{\otimes n}}(1-\phi_n)
\le
\exp\left\{-\frac{n\Delta^2}{2R_s^2}\right\}.
\]
Hence, once the first bound is at most \(\alpha\),
\[
\beta_n^\star(\alpha;Q,\Gamma,r^{(n)})
\ge 1-e^{-c(Q,\Gamma)n},
\qquad
c(Q,\Gamma)=\frac{\Delta^2}{2R_s^2}>0.
\]
This handles the full composite null uniformly. Independence is essential to this particular Hoeffding proof; identical null factors are not required.

At and above the boundary, \(q^{\otimes n}\) is itself an admissible null. Therefore every feasible test has alternative power at most \(\alpha\), while \(\phi\equiv\alpha\) attains \(\alpha\):
\[
\beta_n^\star(\alpha;Q,\Gamma,r^{(n)})=\alpha
\quad\text{for every }n.
\]

For \(m=2\),
\[
\widetilde\Gamma^\star
=\frac{Q(2)}{Q(1)}
=\frac{q_2}{1-q_2},
\]
the success odds for the pair sign/McNemar representation. Pair compatibility is exact.

Do not call \(c(Q,\Gamma)\) a Chernoff exponent or claim it is optimal. The proof establishes an explicit positive exponential lower bound on attainable power, not a matching error exponent.

## Actionable proto/core directive

Add these nodes.

1. `ass:replicated-conditional-design`

   State that there are fixed \(m\ge2\), a fixed record type \(r_0\), fixed scores
   \[
   0<Q(1)\le\cdots\le Q(m),\qquad Q(1)<Q(m),
   \]
   fixed \(\Gamma\ge1\), and fixed \(0<\alpha<1\), such that for every \(n\):

   - \(m_i=m,\ r_i=r_0,\ \Gamma_i=\Gamma\);
   - \(Q_i(j;r_0)=Q(j)\);
   - under the conditional alternative, \(Z_1,\ldots,Z_n\) are i.i.d. with \(q_j=Q(j)/\sum_kQ(k)\);
   - under the conditional sharp-null sensitivity model, the admissible laws are exactly
     \[
     \left\{\bigotimes_{i=1}^np_i:p_i\in\mathcal P_m(\Gamma)\right\}.
     \]

   Explicitly say that null factors may vary with \(i\). This prevents an accidental i.i.d.-null restriction.

2. `def:local-rosenbaum-polytope`

   \[
   \mathcal P_m(\Gamma)
   =\{p\in\Delta_m:p_j/p_k\le\Gamma\ \forall j,k\}.
   \]
   Prefer the division-free form \(p_j\le\Gamma p_k\); it also makes positivity follow from normalization and the all-pairs inequalities.

3. `def:replicated-frontier`

   \[
   \beta_{n,\mathrm{rep}}^\star(\alpha;Q,\Gamma)
   =
   \sup_{\phi:[m]^n\to[0,1]}
   \left\{
   E_{q^{\otimes n}}\phi:
   \sup_{p_i\in\mathcal P_m(\Gamma)}
   E_{\otimes_i p_i}\phi\le\alpha
   \right\}.
   \]
   Note that this is the specialization of the existing `def:frontier` at \(r^{(n)}=(r_0,\ldots,r_0)\), not a competing estimand.

4. `def:all-tests-design-sensitivity`

   \[
   \widetilde\Gamma_{\mathrm{all}}^\star(Q)
   =
   \sup\left\{\Gamma\ge1:
   \lim_{n\to\infty}\beta_{n,\mathrm{rep}}^\star
   (\alpha;Q,\Gamma)=1\right\}.
   \]
   Under \(Q(m)>Q(1)\), the defining set is nonempty. State that the result is independent of the chosen fixed \(\alpha\in(0,1)\).

5. `thm:replicated-all-tests-design-sensitivity`

   State:
   \[
   \widetilde\Gamma_{\mathrm{all}}^\star(Q)
   =\frac{Q(m)}{Q(1)}.
   \]
   More precisely:

   - If \(1\le\Gamma<Q(m)/Q(1)\), there are \(c(Q,\Gamma)>0\) and \(N(\alpha,Q,\Gamma)\) such that
     \[
     \beta_{n,\mathrm{rep}}^\star(\alpha;Q,\Gamma)
     \ge1-e^{-c(Q,\Gamma)n},\qquad n\ge N.
     \]
   - If \(\Gamma\ge Q(m)/Q(1)\), then
     \[
     \beta_{n,\mathrm{rep}}^\star(\alpha;Q,\Gamma)=\alpha
     \quad\forall n.
     \]
   - If \(m=2\), the boundary equals \(q_2/(1-q_2)\).

   Dependencies should include `ass:replicated-conditional-design`, `def:local-rosenbaum-polytope`, `def:replicated-frontier`, and `def:all-tests-design-sensitivity`. If the repeated-design assumption is instead derived from the causal setup, also declare the alternative factorization, common-support, conditioning-support, conditional-independence, Rosenbaum-odds, alternative-law, and frontier nodes explicitly.

## Existing dependency repairs

For `prop:pair-reduction`, change the statement to begin:

> Under `ass:sharp-null`, `ass:conditional-independence`, `ass:rosenbaum-odds`, `ass:alternative-prior-factorization`, `ass:alternative-record-factorization`, `ass:likelihood-common-support`, `ass:conditioning-support`, and `ass:causal-likelihood-separation`, if \(m_i=2\) for every \(i\), then …

Add all of those nodes to `depends_on`. Retain the directly used definition and theorem dependencies. Remove `ass:causal-likelihood-order`: in pairs, positivity plus endpoint separation already implies \(Q_i(2)>Q_i(1)\).

For `thm:set-mixture-lp-characterization`:

- Remove `ass:causal-likelihood-order` from both the statement and `depends_on`.
- Replace unconditional descriptions of \(\mathcal B\) as “\(Q\)-monotone” by “indexed upper-set” or “upper-set candidate.”
- Add a sentence: “When `ass:causal-likelihood-order` additionally holds, this indexed family is the \(Q\)-monotone upper-set family.”
- Preserve every LP, KKT/complementary-slackness, validity-region, affine-envelope, \(m=3\), and \(1/720\) result unchanged.

## Required related-work sentences

Add explicit relevance sentences along these lines:

> Gastwirth, Krieger, and Rosenbaum establish asymptotic separability of worst-case sensitivity calculations for specified rank procedures with multiple controls; the replicated theorem here instead takes the envelope over all conditional randomized tests and identifies the sharp oracle detectability boundary for a fixed one-set alternative. Their result is therefore a computational asymptotic for selected procedures, whereas ours is an information-theoretic benchmark for the entire test class. [Paper](https://faculty.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2012/04/Asymptotic.pdf)

> Zhao studies the pair-matched sensitivity value and proves its asymptotic normality, with applications to power and study design; our pair formula \(q_2/(1-q_2)\) is instead the population all-tests ceiling under a fixed replicated conditional alternative and does not provide a sampling distribution or estimator of the sensitivity value. [Article](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/01621459.2018.1429277)

> Cohen, Olson, and Fogarty optimize design sensitivity over a class of coherent combinations of prespecified outcome statistics; the present benchmark optimizes over every randomized assignment test for a specified conditional alternative, so it is a broader oracle ceiling but not a practical coherent-statistic construction. [Article](https://academic.oup.com/biomet/article/107/4/809/5850657)

> Fogarty’s tilted analysis supplies closed-form limiting worst-case laws and design sensitivities for tilted versions of candidate statistics with multiple controls; our theorem supplies a different object—the maximal achievable common-\(\Gamma\) boundary over all tests—and should be used as an oracle comparator, not claimed to dominate tilted procedures operationally. [Preprint](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.09736)

These sentences directly resolve the missing-relevance finding.

## Positioning and scope changes

Replace the finite-only TL;DR with:

> The finite component gives the exact conditional all-tests LP, least-favorable support criteria, validity regions, and support switches. Under an explicit repeated conditional matched-set design, the asymptotic component identifies the sharp common-\(\Gamma\) all-tests design-sensitivity ceiling \(Q(m)/Q(1)\): below it, uniformly valid tests attain power \(1-e^{-cn}\); at and above it, the alternative is itself an admissible null and the exact frontier is \(\alpha\).

In project justification, identify the field contribution as the combination of:

- finite exact all-tests frontier and least-favorable certificates; and
- a sharp repeated-design oracle boundary with exponential achievability and an exact impossibility result.

In interpretation, say that the separating score is an existence witness and may depend on \((Q,\Gamma)\). It is not claimed to be the classical tilt, a selected rank statistic, a coherent combination, or a practically estimable score when \(Q\) is unknown.

Replace the relevant honest-scope exclusion with:

> The note now derives an asymptotic all-tests design-sensitivity parameter only for the explicit fixed-\(m\), fixed-record, fixed-\(Q\), common-\(\Gamma\), independent-replica conditional design. It proves a positive exponential achievability bound below the boundary and exact power \(\alpha\) at and above it. It does not identify an optimal Chernoff exponent, treat heterogeneous or growing set sizes, estimate \(Q\), or establish unconditional repeated-sampling validity.

Continue verbatim in substance:

> No two-sided \(0.158\) improvement, equality with Bonferroni doubling at \(\Gamma=1\), or equivalence to a statistic-choice program is claimed, because no supported two-sided construction or comparator theorem is present.

This theorem is elementary once the correct repeated experiment is formulated, but it is not merely another finite-duality corollary: it supplies a sharp limit boundary, a uniform exponential rate statement, and an exact converse over all tests. That is plausibly field-tier at D0 if the paper consistently calls it an oracle benchmark and does not market the separation/Hoeffding proof itself as the novelty.
